summary: Updated tools/frank.py by pulling a small upstream change from projectPiki/pikmin. The script now detects when a vanilla object file contains no code-size magic marker, writes the vanilla bytes directly to the target, and exits cleanly instead of continuing into code-size patching logic that would operate on a missing marker.
what_changed: Imported sys so the script can terminate early with sys.exit(0).
Cached vanilla_bytes.find(CODESIZE_MAGIC) in code_size_magic_idx instead of calling find inline later.
Added a no-code-object path: if CODESIZE_MAGIC is not present in the vanilla bytes, frank.py writes the unmodified vanilla bytes to args.target and exits successfully.
Moved reading and closing args.profile until after the missing-CODESIZE_MAGIC check, avoiding unnecessary profile processing when the vanilla object requires no modification.

Changed code_size_offset calculation from vanilla_bytes.find(CODESIZE_MAGIC) + 12 to code_size_magic_idx + len(CODESIZE_MAGIC), making the offset depend on the named marker length rather than a repeated magic number.
smart_moves: Handled the missing-marker case explicitly before doing replacement and code-size parsing, preventing brittle behavior when the object contains no code.
Preserved the vanilla object unchanged for no-code files, matching the comment that such objects require no modification.
Used len(CODESIZE_MAGIC) instead of hard-coded 12, reducing duplication and keeping the offset tied to the marker definition.
Delayed profile file reading until it is actually needed, which keeps the early-exit path simpler and avoids unnecessary I/O.
decomp_lessons: Tooling that patches object bytes should guard against sentinel-byte searches returning -1 before using the result in offsets.
No-code object files can legitimately lack markers expected in code-bearing objects; build/decomp helper scripts should treat that as a normal case, not an error path.
When syncing helper scripts from related decomp projects, keep the change small and traceable to the upstream commit when possible.
Prefer deriving byte offsets from named byte-sequence lengths rather than duplicating literal sizes.
naming_conventions: CODESIZE_MAGIC names the byte sequence used to locate the code-size field.
code_size_magic_idx is used for the index returned by vanilla_bytes.find(CODESIZE_MAGIC).
code_size_offset, code_size_bytes, and code_size retain a clear staged naming pattern for locating, slicing, and decoding the code-size value.
vanilla_bytes, profile_bytes, and stripped_bytes distinguish original object bytes, profile object bytes, and profile bytes after removing the known byte sequence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The script operates at byte level on object files, searching for CODESIZE_MAGIC and decoding the following 4 bytes as a big-endian code size.
The update avoids attempting code-size patching when CODESIZE_MAGIC is absent, which is important for object files with no code sections.

The script continues to strip the byte sequence b"\x48\x00\x00\x01\x60\x00\x00\x00" from profile bytes before comparing or using code-section data, although the broader matching purpose is only implied by the diff.
follow_up_queries: Find later changes to tools/frank.py that build on the no-code-object handling.
Search for other tooling scripts that call bytes.find on magic markers without checking for -1.
Look for PRs that imported or synchronized tooling from projectPiki/pikmin.
Search for CODESIZE_MAGIC usage across the repository.
